reviews.review_description: Not responding.....

developer_response.body: Sorry to hear you are having problems. Download and install are handled by the Oculus app, so I don't have any control over that. I did just delete my install and download it to my Galaxy S8 without any problems though. Perhaps you would consider removing your 1 star "review" until you have been able to download and play the app?

reviews.review_description:
Man oh man. This is as close as I'll ever get to actually thrust through space.

Does everyone know about the graphics settings? Turn it UP! All the way up!

I'm on Oculus Go. 

Nothing negative to say at all. Just plain, outright FANTASTIC!

I've had my VR legs since October of 2016 but I gotta say... turning off comfort setting really messed with said VR legs. I look forward to getting my Spacewalk VR legs now.

Much kudos for a job most excellent!

developer_response.body: That's really encouraging - thank you! Even as the developer, I don't fly with the auto-stabiliser off. Certainly not if I want to walk straight later :-))

reviews.review_title: Best Being in Space App on GearVR
reviews.review_description:
After you are sick of roller coasters and jump scares go get some overview effect! Start slow and soon you will be... 

"floating in a most peculiar way
And the stars look very different today

For here am I, sitting in a tin can
Far above the world
Planet Earth is blue
And there's nothing I can do

Though I'm past one hundred thousand miles
I'm feeling very still
And I think my spaceship knows which way to go..."

David Bowie (RIP) Space Oddity

developer_response.body:
:-)))
Only a few million more installs and I'll be able to license David Bowie's Space Oddity to go with it! Thanks for the very encouraging review.

reviews.review_description: If you want the feeling of a gravity free environment while being in close earth orbit then this is it. This is a free download for the basics with the option to purchase the full app. I think it took me all of about 2 minutes to decide to buy it. Wonderful. Do more.

developer_response.body: Thanks very much for the really encouraging review. :-)

reviews.review_description: we have no problemo my friend shining all away

I loved the concept, but execution leaves a lot to be desired. Graphics are so-so, music is cliché, and UI and controls are not very smooth. There isn't a lot to do as well except to wander in empty space or playing two minigames (at three difficulty levels).

Update (response to developer): I was already using the highest graphics quality on the Oculus Go, but textures, meshes and shaders are not comparable to similar apps (e.g., Titans of Space, Space Time, Neos: The Universe, Mission:ISS, ...). Sorry for not being clear on the "smoothness" problem, but it is not related to dropped frames: changing the shuttle orbit should have a smooth/animated transition instead of a direct cut (it would look much better and not cause disorientation); most of the time the UI rapidly "flickers" when I try to select a list item (such as a challenge), perhaps due to the app sensing a "scroll" when I just rest my thumb on the Oculus Go controller (this alone made me give up on the app). I also found the UI targets (such as buttons or list items) too small, while the overall circular interface is too big. Opening and closing the interface could also be faster.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the review. In the control panel, you can turn off the music if it is not to your taste. You can also turn up the graphics quality (at this risk of hitting hardware limitations).  From version 4.1.53, there are improvements to how the laser pointer interacts with the interface which should fix the issues you noted with flickering. Regarding image quality, this app is primarily about the view of the Earth and around 80% of the processing power goes to this. The other apps you mentioned either don't allow changing the view of The Earth ( which means they can use a pre-rendered image) or they don't have the detail that this app does (moving clouds, lighting angle on cloud peaks, large area view of surface etc). Those apps don't need this as they're about different things. Thanks again for feeding back. Feel free to contact me via forthinteract.com. :-)

reviews.review_description: Virtual environment for space-floating simulation. TRT 5 min demo.

developer_response.body: Thanks very much for the 5 stars. The free version allows any number of 5 minute flights before being recalled back to the shuttle. The in-app upgrade removes that limitation.

reviews.review_description: I love this app so much!! The free floating experience is amazing. Graphics are wonderful. One of my favorite apps to date.

developer_response.body: Thank you sooooo much. It's great to hear when people are enjoying my app :-)

reviews.review_title: I loved it until you have to pay for it
reviews.review_description: Free means free not just 13 mins. Of the app

developer_response.body: Hello. Glad to hear you loved it, but I think you might have missed something about the free version limit. As mentioned in the product description, the free version allows you to fly for 5 minutes each time. You can have as many 5 minute flights as you like. The upgrade removes the 5 minute flight limit. Nothing else is limited. So please, keep spacewalking :-)

reviews.review_description: Love this simply amazing

developer_response.body: Thanks very much :-) It's great to hear about people enjoying Spacewalk VR

reviews.review_description: Won't let me download it (I do have enough space)

developer_response.body: Hello. Sorry to hear that. Unfortunately, I don't have any control over the download and install process, as this is entirely done by Oculus (Meta). If you can't download it from Meta, this software is also available via SideQuest and Itch, although I'll probably get into trouble with Meta for saying that :-)

reviews.review_description: Great app, 5 stars!!!!! I will recommend this to all my friends!

I BOUGHT THIS ON OCULUS GO AND LOVED IT.  NOW THEY ARE TELLING ME I NEED TO BUY IT AGAIN FOR MY OCULUS QUEST DEVICE. IT SHOULD BE FREE IF YOU HAVE ALREADY PURCHASED IT, OR YOU SHOULD BE ABLE TO GET A REFUND. WE ARE BEING RIPPED OFF  :-(

developer_response.body:
Hello,
Firstly I am glad you loved my app on your Oculus Go:-)
This app is currently only available for Gear and Go. It is not available for Quest at all, so are you sure you were asked to re-buy THIS app for Quest?
Perhaps you would consider changing your one-star review as you said you loved the app on your Go. This damages my rating, but I can't control Oculus' policy about Go apps being available / compatible with Quest.

reviews.review_description: This is a review

I am impressed, this app is based on my favourite subject. On the Oculus Go this runs well, the graphics set to high. I turn the music off, just enjoy the view and the feeling of freedom floating in space. Would welcome any updates, but very good just as it stands.

developer_response.body: Thanks very much for the positive review. This experience is all about the free-floating feeling so I'm glad you're enjoying it :-)

reviews.review_description: It's fun to try for a few minutes but don't expect too much from it. It's a very basic app with very average graphics. There are similar apps (also free) that look better.

reviews.review_title: The kind of experience VR was made for!
reviews.review_description:
I'm playing on Oculus Go. This is, by far, the best VR space experience I've tried. I truly felt like an astronaut on a space walk. That's not to say it's easy. I set the game to simulate true weightlessness, which is both difficult to control and disorienting. I wouldn't suggest this setting if you don't yet have good VR legs. I tend to love games that make others motion sick. This isn't the kind of motion sickness of a flawed experience. Rather, it's what you would expect a real space walk to feel like. I find it exhilarating! This is the kind of experience, for most of us, only possible in VR. I was happy to pay to unlock the full experience. I imagine it will take quite a bit of time to master. There's plenty of missions to test your spacewalking skills that record your best time. 

One thing I would love to see in this app is support for a gamepad. I'm not a huge fan of having to turn my head to change thrusters. It's a minor gripe. As it is now, this is almost perfect and would be if I could control everything with a gamepad keeping my head free to gaze at what I want.

developer_response.body: Thanks very, very much for such a detailed and positive review. It's really uplifting to hear of people enjoying the Spacewalk VR Experience. I spend most of the time trying to make the experience as real as possible, within the limits of running on mobile devices of course. It was interesting coming up with controls that felt real yet avoided motion sickness as much as is possible for a free-floating, weightless experience. I found that spinning on the spot was fine, tumbling forward and backwards was fine when you get used to it, but rolling side to side always led to an uncomfortable experience (which is why you can't do that). Thanks again. I will look into GamePad controls. :-)

reviews.review_description: It runs great on the Go. It's fun and challenging. Also educational. Everyone should try it
I really want to see this but Oculus Go controller wont move on to the instructions or the next button. Wont it work with my controller as to looks nothing like the one it shows.

My Oculus Go controller does not work.  I get into the shuttle bay but cannot continue.   The controller will not click the buttons to allow me to proceed.
  Thank you for the response, that gets you another star.  The answer is, yes I can see my laser pointer and move it around, but it go through the buttons and will not click on the buttons so I can proceed.  I believe my review would be updated even more if I could get into and explore the app.

developer_response.body: Try updating to the latest version which has just been uploaded. This will say v4.0.80.1 on the splash screen. I couldn't directly see why you couldn't click an interface button, but this version has updated Oculus libraries in case something changed there.
